---
title: "Audit Target: mv_biomass_composition"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Executive Summary

The data quality audit for the `mv_biomass_composition` dataset reveals significant systemic issues that compromise the reliability of the compositional profiles. While the dataset has expanded from 760 to 6,366 records, this growth is accompanied by a severe lack of metadata traceability—specifically, a 100% missing rate for analyst names and high attrition in analyst emails. The most critical technical findings include physically impossible negative values (min: -244.0) and extreme outliers (max: 161,000) that suggest severe unit conversion errors or uncorrected instrumental drift in the measurement of glucose/xylose yields and solids.

Anomalies are concentrated in grape pomace and almond processing byproducts (hulls and shells). The high frequency of 'LOW' severity flags for structural carbohydrates (xylan, lignin) in almond hulls suggests a systematic shift in either the sample population or the analytical protocol (e.g., NREL LAP deviations) compared to the reference set. Furthermore, the presence of negative values for 'observed_value' indicates a failure in blanking or baseline subtraction processes during HPLC or ICP-OES analysis, necessitating a full review of the laboratory's QC procedures for those batches.

## Evidently Profile

<iframe src="../evidently/mv_biomass_composition.html" width="100%" height="800px" frameborder="0"></iframe>

## Custom Visuals

Links to specific analysis plots:

- [Biomass Composition Plots](../../../analysis/composition/)
- [Conversion Distribution Plots](../../../analysis/conversion/)

:::
